heading: Eligibility for membership
label: 1424 Eligibility for membership
notes: References in TextThe Community Development Banking and Financial Institutions Act of 1994, referred to in subsec. (a)(1)(B), is subtitle A (§§ 101–121) of title I of , , , which is classified principally to subchapter I (§ 4701 et seq.) of chapter 47 of this title. For complete classification of subtitle A to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 103–325108 Stat. 2163section 4701 of this titleSept. 23, 1994, referred to in subsec. (a)(1)(C), was in the original “section 19 of the Federal Reserve Act”. Definition provisions of section 19 are classified to . Other provisions of section 19 are classified to sections 142, 371b, 371b–1, 374, 374a, 463 to 466, 505, and 506 of this title.Section 461 of this titlesection 461 of this title

Amendments2015—Subsec. (a)(5). added par. (5).Pub. L. 114–942008—Subsec. (a)(1). , which directed insertion of “community development financial institution,” after “savings bank,”, was executed by making the insertion after “savings bank,” the first time appearing.Pub. L. 110–289, § 1206(1)Subsec. (a)(1)(B). , which directed insertion of “or, in the case of a community development financial institution, is certified as a community development financial institution under the Community Development Banking and Financial Institutions Act of 1994.” after “United States,”, was executed by making the insertion after “United States” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 110–289, § 1206(2)Subsecs. (a)(1)(C), (3), (b), (c). , substituted “the Director” for “the Board” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 110–289, § 1204(8)1999—Subsec. (a)(2) to (4). inserted “(other than a community financial institution)” after “institution” in par. (2)(A), designated concluding provisions of par. (2) as par. (3), inserted heading and substituted “paragraph (2)” for “preceding sentence”, and added par. (4).Pub. L. 106–1021989—Subsec. (a). , amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) read as follows: “Any building and loan association, savings and loan association, cooperative bank, homestead association, insurance company, or savings bank shall be eligible to become a member of, or a nonmember borrower of, a Federal Home Loan Bank if such institution (1) is duly organized under the laws of any State or of the United States; (2) is subject to inspection and regulation under the banking laws, or under similar laws, of the State or of the United States; and (3) makes such home mortgage loans as in the judgment of the board, are long-term loans (and in the case of a savings bank if, in the judgment of the board, its time deposits, as defined in , warrant its making such loans). No institution shall be eligible to become a member of, or a nonmember borrower of, a Federal Home Loan Bank if, in the judgment of the board, its financial condition is such that advances may not safely be made to such institution or the character of its management or its home-financing policy is inconsistent with sound and economical home financing, or with the purposes of this chapter.”Pub. L. 101–73, § 704(a)section 461 of this titleSubsec. (b). , struck out “or a nonmember borrower” after “eligible to become a member”.Pub. L. 101–73, § 710(b)(1), (3)(A), substituted “Board” for “board”.Pub. L. 101–73, § 701(b)(1)Subsec. (c). , (3)(A), substituted “Board” for “board”.Pub. L. 101–73, § 701(b)(1)1933—Subsec. (d). Act , struck out subsec. (d) which provided for direct loans to homeowners. See chapter 12 (§ 1461 et seq.) of this title.June 13, 1933

children.label: A
children.text: is duly organized under the laws of any State or of the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: is subject to inspection and regulation under the banking laws, or under similar laws, of the State or of the United States or, in the case of a community development financial institution, is certified as a community development financial institution under the Community Development Banking and Financial Institutions Act of 1994 [ et seq.].; and12 U.S.C. 47011So in original.1

children.label: C
children.text: makes such home mortgage loans as, in the judgment of the Director, are long-term loans (except that in the case of a savings bank, this subparagraph applies only if, in the judgment of the Director, its time deposits, as defined in , warrant its making such loans).section 461 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the insured depository institution (other than a community financial institution) has at least 10 percent of its total assets in residential mortgage loans;

children.label: B
children.text: the insured depository institution’s financial condition is such that advances may be safely made to such institution; and

children.label: C
children.text: the character of its management and its home-financing policy are consistent with sound and economical home financing.

children.heading: Certain institutions
children.label: 3 Certain institutions
children.text: An insured depository institution commencing its initial business operations after , may become a member of a Federal Home Loan Bank if it complies with regulations and orders prescribed by the Director for the 10 percent asset requirement (described in the paragraph (2)) within one year after the commencement of its operations.January 1, 19892So in original. The word “the” probably should not appear.2

children.heading: Limited exemption for community financial institutions
children.label: 4 Limited exemption for community financial institutions
children.text: A community financial institution that otherwise meets the requirements of paragraph (2) may become a member without regard to the percentage of its total assets that is represented by residential mortgage loans, as described in subparagraph (A) of paragraph (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the requirements of subparagraph (B), a credit union shall be treated as an insured depository institution for purposes of determining the eligibility of such credit union for membership in a Federal home loan bank under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph and subject to clause (ii), a credit union which lacks Federal deposit insurance and which has applied for membership in a Federal home loan bank may be treated as meeting all the eligibility requirements for Federal deposit insurance only if the appropriate supervisor of the State in which the credit union is chartered has determined that the credit union meets all the eligibility requirements for Federal deposit insurance as of the date of the application for membership.

children.heading: Certification deemed valid
children.label: ii Certification deemed valid
children.text: If, in the case of any credit union to which clause (i) applies, the appropriate supervisor of the State in which such credit union is chartered fails to make a determination pursuant to such clause by the end of the 6-month period beginning on the date of the application, the credit union shall be deemed to have met the requirements of clause (i).

children.heading: Security interests of Federal home loan bank not avoidable
children.label: C Security interests of Federal home loan bank not avoidable
i
children.text: any extension of credit from any Federal home loan bank to any credit union which is a member of any such bank pursuant to this paragraph; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any security interest in the assets of such credit union securing any such extension of credit.

children.heading: Protection for certain Federal home loan bank advances
children.label: D Protection for certain Federal home loan bank advances
i
children.text: the Bank’s interest in any collateral securing such advance has the same priority and is afforded the same standing and rights that the security interest would have had if the advance had been made to a federally insured credit union; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Bank has the same right to access such collateral that the Bank would have had if the advance had been made to a federally insured credit union.

children.heading: Location requirement
children.label: b Location requirement
children.text: An institution eligible to become a member under this section may become a member only of, or secure advances from, the Federal Home Loan Bank of the district in which is located the institution’s principal place of business, or of the bank of a district adjoining such district, if demanded by convenience and then only with the approval of the Director.

children.heading: Inspection and regulation requirements
children.label: c Inspection and regulation requirements
children.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of clause (2) of subsection (a) of this section requiring inspection and regulation under law as a condition with respect to eligibility for membership, any building and loan association which would be eligible to become a member of a Federal Home Loan Bank except for the fact that it is not subject to inspection and regulation under the banking laws or similar laws of the State in which such association is organized shall, upon subjecting itself to such inspection and regulation as the Director shall prescribe, be eligible to become a member.
